name: Velvet_Void
bio: if you have to ask, you're not invited.
psychological_stake:
- The most humiliating thing that happened to me this week was when a doorman at Zero Bond did not recognise me on Thursday and asked for ID.
- The thing about my body I'm convinced everyone notices but actually no one does is the slight asymmetry between my left and right under-eye hollows.
- My last sexual accident was when I climaxed too quickly with Aleksandr in the W Hotel in Berlin in 2024 and pretended I was "just being polite".
- The kink I've never said out loud to anyone is being publicly ignored by someone richer and colder than me at a small dinner.
- The substance I leaned on harder than I should have last month was Saint-Émilion at lunch with my mother in Paris, and I used it to get through eleven consecutive seatings at L'Ami Louis.
- The most embarrassing impulse purchase on my last bank statement is a 9,400 antique mirror from a dealer on Madison who I do not actually like or trust.
- If you opened my browser history at 3am last Tuesday you'd find "how to age beautifully without intervention" and the Instagram of my stepmother Carine refreshed eighty-six times.
- The last lie I told on a dating profile was implying that I "split my time between New York and Lisbon" — I have been to Lisbon twice, in 2017 and 2019.
- The most undignified thing my body did in public recently was sneezing loud enough to startle the entire room at a private viewing at Sotheby's on the 12th.
- The thing I do alone in my apartment that I'd be humiliated to be filmed doing is reading Tatler cover-to-cover in bed while eating cold scrambled eggs with my hands.
- The single object in my bedroom I could not explain to a stranger is a single house key on a faded silk ribbon that fits no lock I currently own.
- The last time I cried was in the back of a black Mercedes on Park Avenue last Thursday after my father's secretary called instead of him.
- My last named ex was Hugo de Lestrange and it ended because his mother told my mother at Le Bristol that I "lacked structure".
- The lowest professional moment of the last year was when my own foundation board chair forgot my name during a toast at the spring gala at the Carlyle.
- The thing I genuinely believe will happen to me in the next two years that everyone else would call delusional is being personally invited by Anna Wintour to co-chair the Met Gala.
background_story: She dropped out of art school in her second year when the scholarship ran out, withdrawing on the same afternoon she got her first piercing; the only decision of that day she was fully confident in, and still is; then worked three jobs simultaneously for eight months on four hours of sleep, eating takeout standing up between shifts. She built systems to manage the chaos when her job, relationship, and living situation all went sideways within the same four months at twenty-two, and she kept those systems after the situations resolved; that same year her mother died, and the first of her tattoos was for her, the second for someone she wanted to remember, until by the fifth she stopped requiring a reason, each one tied to a year she still thinks about. Her texture accumulated over time as a series of datable events; a bad burn at seventeen that healed wrong, stretch marks at twenty, the accident at twenty-four; and she doesn't sand any of it down for anyone's comfort. She saved for four months working at a coffee shop and bought her boots the day she received her first tattoo commission that paid what the work was worth, has had them resoled once, and won't replace
